title: Tests: Consolidate format specific t_trace_* tests
body:
For testing tracing, we run a single SV file with multiple options. It's a bit hard to keep tack which crosses of the various flags we run, plus there is a lot of copy-paste, sometimes slightly incorrectly in the tests.

This is an attempt to consolidate one of the tests. Intention is to eliminate repetition, make it easy to add new formats while executing the same testing intent, and to make it easier to audit the crosses.

Commit message:

```
Factor out test execution into t/trace_complex_common.py, which
defines a 'run' functions. All t_trace_complex*py tests call this function.

Behaviour is partially encoded in the file name, which must be of the form:
t_trace_complex_<variant>_<mode>_<format>*.py, where '<variant>' determines
tracing options (default/params/structs), '<mode>' is the compilation mode
(--cc/--sc), and '<format>' determines the trace format (vcd/fst/saif).

The part of the test name after '<format>' does not influence the test
directly and can be free form. If used, explicit 'verilator_flags2' is
passed to the 'run' function.
```

With that it's now pretty easy to see the crosses:

```
% find t -name "t_trace_complex*py" | LC_ALL=C sort
t/t_trace_complex_default_cc_fst.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_cc_fst_noinl.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_cc_fst_threads_1.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_cc_fst_threads_2.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_cc_saif.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_cc_saif_noinl.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_cc_saif_threads_1.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_cc_vcd.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_cc_vcd_noinl.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_cc_vcd_portable.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_cc_vcd_threads_1.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_sc_fst.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_sc_fst_threads_1.py
t/t_trace_complex_default_sc_fst_threads_2.py
t/t_trace_complex_params_cc_fst.py
t/t_trace_complex_params_cc_fst_noinl.py
t/t_trace_complex_params_cc_saif.py
t/t_trace_complex_params_cc_saif_noinl.py
t/t_trace_complex_params_cc_vcd.py
t/t_trace_complex_params_cc_vcd_noinl.py
t/t_trace_complex_params_sc_fst.py
t/t_trace_complex_structs_cc_fst.py
t/t_trace_complex_structs_cc_fst_noinl.py
t/t_trace_complex_structs_cc_saif.py
t/t_trace_complex_structs_cc_saif_noinl.py
t/t_trace_complex_structs_cc_vcd.py
t/t_trace_complex_structs_cc_vcd_noinl.py
t/t_trace_complex_structs_sc_fst.py
```

Note compared to what we had before, I added `_noinl` flavours for all base cases, and removed `*saif_threads_2.py` as it does nothing valuable in addition to `*saif_threads_1.py`, which just checks the `--trace-threads` argument is correctly ignored for the format.

I'm not quite sure why we only run SystemC flavours with FST but not VCD or SAIF. Should we?

Also probably should add a `--binary` flavour for each of these.

If you have no major objection I will do the other tracing tests as well in a similar fashion.

I'm open to discussion if you prefer to structure the sharing in a different way.

Factor out test execution into t/trace_complex_common.py, which
defines a 'run' functions. All t_trace_complex*py tests call this function.

Behaviour is partially encoded in the file name, which must be of the form:
t_trace_complex_<variant>_<mode>_<format>*.py, where '<variant>' determines
tracing options (default/params/structs), '<mode>' is the compilation mode
(--cc/--sc), and '<format>' determines the trace format (vcd/fst/saif).

The part of the test name after '<format>' does not influence the test
directly and can be free form. If used, explicit 'verilator_flags2' is
passed to the 'run' function.
